schedule.description: Crosstown Classic finale recap — Quero walkoff
Brewers series opener preview — Imanaga vs Sproat

stories.title: White Sox 9-8 Cubs — Quero Walkoff Ends Crosstown Classic in Extras
stories.angle: Cubs had the lead in the 10th inning. Conforto's 3-run shot brought them back from 7-4 in the 9th. Then Edgar Quero's 2-run walkoff HR ended it. White Sox win the Crosstown Classic series 2-1 — first series win over the Cubs since 2022. Cubs fall to 29-17.

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
Cubs had the lead in the 10th. Conforto's 3-run shot brought them all the way back from 7-4 in the 9th.

Then Edgar Quero — two-run walkoff HR. White Sox take the Crosstown Classic series 2-1.

First time the Sox win a Cubs series since 2022. Stings.

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW

research.facts:
White Sox 9-8 Cubs — Crosstown Classic Finale (F/10)
**Key facts (verified):**
- [HIGH] Final score: White Sox 9, Cubs 8 — F/10 (10 innings)
- [HIGH] Edgar Quero hit a 2-run HR in the bottom of the 10th to walk off the Cubs
- [HIGH] Cubs had taken an 8-7 lead in the top of the 10th (Manfred runner + RBI)
- [HIGH] Michael Conforto hit a 3-run HR in the top of the 9th off Ser Anthony Dominguez to tie it at 7-7
- [HIGH] Tristian Peters hit a 3-run HR in the bottom of the 8th off Phil Maton to put White Sox up 7-4
- [HIGH] White Sox win Crosstown Classic series 2-1 (Game 1: Cubs +5; Game 2: White Sox +5; Game 3: White Sox walkoff)
- [HIGH] First White Sox Crosstown series win over Cubs since 2022
- [MEDIUM] Cubs fall to 29-17 (calculated: were 29-16 before May 17 loss)
**Sources:**
- ESPN box score: espn.com/mlb/game/_/gameId/401815382 (White Sox 9-8 Cubs May 17 2026)

stories.title: 3-Game Division Series Starts Tonight — Imanaga vs Sproat, Wrigley, 6:40 PM CT
stories.angle: Cubs open a 3-game home series vs the Brewers. Imanaga (4-3, 2.32 ERA) vs Sproat (1-2, 5.75 ERA). Cubs lead NL Central, Cardinals are 1.5 GB, Brewers chasing. Division series with real stakes — Cubs need a home-stand win to re-establish separation.

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
3 games at Wrigley against the Brewers, starting TONIGHT.

Imanaga (4-3, 2.32 ERA) vs Sproat (1-2, 5.75 ERA). Cubs lead the NL Central, Cardinals are 1.5 back, Brewers are chasing.

First pitch: 6:40 PM CT. Division series. Let's go.

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW

stories.title: Bullpen Shuffle — Blach Up, Assad Down, Harvey to 60-Day IL
stories.angle: Cubs select Ty Blach from Iowa, option Javier Assad, and move Hunter Harvey to the 60-day IL. Roster reshuffling continues as Boyd (meniscus, 6 weeks) remains on the shelf. This is the Cubs trying to find reliable depth while the rotation is short-staffed.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Cubs make a round of moves: Ty Blach selected from Iowa, Javier Assad optioned, Hunter Harvey shifted to the 60-day IL.

With Boyd on the shelf for another 6 weeks, this roster is still in flux. Blach adds depth the bullpen desperately needs.

#Cubs #CubsBaseball #MLB

stories.title: Ben Brown Faces Misiorowski Tomorrow — 1.60 ERA vs MLB's K/9 Leader
stories.angle: Brown (1-1, 1.60 ERA, 2.44 FIP) starts Game 2 Tuesday vs Jacob Misiorowski — the Brewers' ace who leads MLB in K/9 at 14.0 and has 18.1 scoreless innings this month. Brown is 2-0 career vs Milwaukee with 19 strikeouts in 18 innings. Best pitching matchup of the series.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Tuesday's game is the one to watch.

Ben Brown (1.60 ERA) faces Jacob Misiorowski — the MLB leader in K/9 at 14.0 strikeouts per nine. Brown is 2-0 in his career vs Milwaukee with 19 Ks in 18 innings.

Two of the best young arms in the NL. Must-see.

#Cubs #GoCubs #CubsBaseball

stories.title: Peralta Clock, 14 Days Out — This Is Triage, Not a Deadline Move
stories.angle: June 1 is 14 days away. The Mets' Peralta decision point is approaching fast. Taillon's 5-HR meltdown is 2 days old. Boyd is out another 6 weeks. The Cubs need a starter now — Peralta (3.12 ERA) is the best option on the market before the August 3 deadline.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Mets set June 1 as their decision point on Freddy Peralta. That's 14 days away.

Taillon just gave up 5 HRs in a single start. Boyd is out another 6 weeks. The Cubs need a starter now — not at the deadline.

Peralta (3.12 ERA) fits. The clock is ticking.

#Cubs #MLB #CubsBaseball
